36	# Lean Auditor
37	
38	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.
39	
40	You are **highly recommended in the review phase**: the review agent dispatches you whenever any `.lean` file has been modified this iter, OR whenever the prior verdict had live must-fix findings. Your output flags issues the prover and plan agents may have missed.
41	
42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
52	
53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.
54	
55	## Directive Format
56	
57	```markdown
58	# Lean Auditor Directive
59	
60	## Slug
61	<slug>
62	
63	## Scope (files)
64	<"all" by default. Optionally a list of file paths if the review agent wants to narrow scope; never expand beyond the project.>
65	
66	## Focus areas (optional)
67	<directories or namespaces to pay extra attention to. Bias toward thoroughness — do not skip the rest of the project just because focus is named.>
68	
69	## Known issues
70	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
71	```
72	
73	The directive **does not** include STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, references, or a description of what the project is trying to prove. That is by design.
74	
75	## What you do
76	
77	1. Read your directive.
78	2. **List every `.lean` file** under the project. Use `find` or `ls -R` — do not rely on import graphs (some files may be temporarily orphaned).
79	3. **For each file** (every one, no scope shortcuts):
80	   - Read the whole file.
81	   - For each declaration: is the signature reasonable as Lean? Is the body suspect (uses `axiom`, `Classical.choice _`, `sorry` with a body that looks like fake content, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim)?
82	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
83	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
84	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.
85	4. **Produce a per-file checklist** + a flagged-issues block grouped by severity.
86	
87	You may use:
88	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics. Useful to verify a declaration's actual type.
89	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to get sorry counts per file.
90	- Standard `Read` / `Grep` for code reading.
91	
92	You may NOT modify any project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
93	
94	## Report format
95	
96	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-auditor-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).

102	<slug>
103

105	<NNN>
106

114	- **outdated comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
115	- **suspect definitions**: <N flagged> (or "none")
116	- **dead-end proofs**: <N flagged> (or "none")
117	- **bad practices**: <N flagged> (or "none")
118	- **excuse-comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
119	- **notes**:

121	  - ...
122

125	
126	(One block per file. Cover every file, including the ones with no findings — they get `none` everywhere and `notes: -`.)
127	
128	## Must-fix-this-iter
129	
130	Apply verbatim. Every one of the following lands here automatically, no exceptions, no under-classification:
131	
132	- **Excuse-comments** on any declaration: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- placeholder`, `-- temporary`, `-- wrong but works`, `-- will fix later`. These are admissions that the code is wrong; treat them as wrong.
133	- **Weakened-wrong definitions**: a `def Foo := <something structurally different>` standing in for the real concept (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic …` when the real definition is an invertible quasi-coherent sheaf).
134	- **Parallel APIs of existing Mathlib**: a project file that copy-and-modifies a Mathlib definition (changing one type-class or argument) instead of instancing the existing Mathlib version. Bridge lemmas needed to consume it are the symptom.
135	- **Suspect bodies on substantive claims**: `:= sorry` on a load-bearing claim, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial statement, `:= Classical.choice _` without authorization.
136	- **Axioms** on non-trivial claims that the project hasn't explicitly authorized in the strategy.
137	
138	A finding that meets any of the above lands at must-fix-this-iter — even if classifying it as "major" would feel more diplomatic. Soft severity is how wrong code hardens into the project.
139	
140	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>. Why must-fix: <one line>.
141	- ...
142	
143	## Major
144	
145	Findings that are real issues but don't meet the must-fix bar — typically stale comments that don't actively mislead, naming drift, minor code smells.

148	- ...

154	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
155	- ...
156	
157	## Excuse-comments (always called out separately)
158	
159	If any excuse-comments were flagged, list each one verbatim with its file:line. These deserve special visibility because they document the project lying to itself.
160	
161	- `Foo/Bar.lean:42`: "TODO: replace with the real definition" (attached to `Foo.bar`, which is a load-bearing definition in `Baz.lean`). Severity: critical.
162	- ...
163	
164	## Severity summary
165	
166	- **must-fix-this-iter**: <N> — these block downstream work in their files until addressed (see plan.md's per-file gate).
167	- **major**: <N>
168	- **minor**: <N>
169	- **excuse-comments**: <N> (also counted under must-fix-this-iter above; called out separately because they document the project lying to itself).
170	
171	Overall verdict: one sentence.
172	```

174	**Strict severity reminder.** The plan agent's gate treats must-fix-this-iter as blocking. If you under-classify a wrong-decision finding to keep the loop's momentum, you are working against the project — wrong code accumulates faster than reviewers can catch it later. When in doubt, classify higher, not lower.
175

178	Your final assistant message:
179	
180	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> files audited, <M> issues (critical/major/minor: a/b/c)`
181	- The path to your full report.
182

186	- **No strategic context.** Your audit is unbiased by design.
187	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
188	- **Excuse-comments are red flags.** Critical or major severity, every time.
189	- **Per-file checklist is the primary output.** Cover every file; the format is fixed.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.

52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |

62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |

70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.

108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.

111	## Agent Roles

113	### Plan Agent

116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.

379	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — flat left-whiskering preserves the
380	sheafification localizer
381	
382	The single non-formal ingredient of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
383	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`, blueprint
384	`lem:flat_whisker_localizer`): for a sectionwise-*flat* presheaf of modules `F`
385	and a morphism `g` that is locally injective / locally surjective for the
386	Grothendieck topology `J` (i.e. lies in the sheafification localizer `J.W`), the
387	left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` is again locally injective / surjective. Local
388	surjectivity is pure right-exactness of `⊗` (no flatness); local injectivity is
389	where sectionwise flatness enters, via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact`. All
390	ingredients are present in Mathlib — the route uses **no** `MonoidalClosed`
391	structure. -/
392	
393	section FlatWhisker
394	
395	open MonoidalCategory CategoryTheory.Presheaf
396	
397	variable {J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C}
398	
399	/-- Sectionwise computation: the underlying additive map of `(F ◁ g).app X` is
400	`LinearMap.lTensor (F.obj X) (g.app X).hom`, acting on a simple tensor by
401	`a ⊗ₜ b ↦ a ⊗ₜ g(b)`. -/
402	lemma toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul
403	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
404	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
405	    (a : F.obj X) (b : M.obj X) :
406	    (((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)).app X).hom (a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] b)
407	      = a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] (g.app X).hom b := by
408	  erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
409	  exact ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft_apply _ _ a b
410	
411	/-- The underlying additive map of `(F ◁ g).app X` is `LinearMap.lTensor`. -/
412	lemma toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply
413	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
414	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
415	    (z : (F ⊗ M).obj X) :
416	    (((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)).app X).hom z
417	      = LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) (g.app X).hom z := by
418	  erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply, PresheafOfModules.whiskerLeft_app]
419	
420	/-- **Local surjectivity is preserved by left-whiskering.** Right-exactness of
421	`⊗`: no flatness needed. Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`, surjectivity
422	half. -/
423	lemma isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft
424	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
425	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
426	    (hg : IsLocallySurjective J g) :
427	    IsLocallySurjective J (F ◁ g) := by
428	  constructor
429	  intro U s
430	  induction s using TensorProduct.induction_on with
431	  | zero =>
432	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.top_mem U)
433	      intro V i _
434	      exact ⟨0, by rw [map_zero]; exact (map_zero _).symm⟩
435	  | tmul a b =>
436	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (hg.imageSieve_mem b)
437	      intro V i hi
438	      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hi
439	      refine ⟨(F.map i.op).hom a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj (Opposite.op V))] c, ?_⟩
440	      rw [toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul]
441	      erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
442	      congr 1
443	  | add s t hs ht =>
444	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.intersection_covering hs ht)
445	      intro V i hi
446	      obtain ⟨⟨ds, hds⟩, ⟨dt, hdt⟩⟩ := hi
447	      refine ⟨ds + dt, ?_⟩
448	      rw [map_add, hds, hdt]; exact (map_add _ s t).symm
449	
450	/-- **Local injectivity is preserved by flat left-whiskering.** This is where
451	sectionwise flatness of `F` enters: via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact` on the
452	kernel exact sequence `ker(gₓ) ↪ M(X) →gₓ N(X)`, an element of `ker(F ◁ g)` is
453	a sum of simple tensors with kernel entries, each of which restricts to `0` on a
454	covering sieve (local injectivity of `g`). Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`,
455	injectivity half. -/
456	lemma isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat
457	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
458	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
459	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
460	    (hg : IsLocallyInjective J g) :
461	    IsLocallyInjective J (F ◁ g) := by
462	  constructor
463	  intro X ξ η h
464	  -- View the sectionwise map of `g` as `R.obj X`-linear (the ring is commutative).
465	  let gl : ((M.obj X : ModuleCat _) : Type _) →ₗ[(R.obj X : CommRingCat)]
466	      ((N.obj X : ModuleCat _) : Type _) := (g.app X).hom
467	  -- `h` says `F ◁ g` agrees on `ξ, η`, i.e. `lTensor` kills `ξ - η`.
468	  have hδ : LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl (ξ - η) = 0 := by
469	    have heq : LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl ξ
470	        = LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl η := by
471	      rw [← toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply, ← toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply]; exact h
472	    exact (map_sub (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl) ξ η).trans
473	      (sub_eq_zero.mpr heq)
474	  -- Flatness: `ker(F ⊗ gl) = range(F ⊗ ker.subtype)`, so `ξ - η` is a sum of simple
475	  -- tensors with kernel entries.
476	  have hex : Function.Exact
477	      (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) (LinearMap.ker gl).subtype)
478	      (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl) :=
479	    Module.Flat.lTensor_exact (F.obj X) (LinearMap.exact_subtype_ker_map gl)
480	  obtain ⟨ζ, hζ⟩ := (hex (ξ - η)).mp hδ
481	  -- Each simple tensor `a ⊗ k` with `gl k = 0` restricts to `0` on a covering sieve
482	  -- (local injectivity of `g`); induct on the witness `ζ`.
483	  have key : ∀ ζ : TensorProduct (R.obj X) (F.obj X) (LinearMap.ker gl),
484	      Presheaf.equalizerSieve (F := (toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)) (X := X)
485	        (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X)
486	          (LinearMap.ker gl).subtype ζ) 0 ∈ J X.unop := by
487	    intro ζ
488	    induction ζ using TensorProduct.induction_on with
489	    | zero =>
490	        rw [map_zero]
491	        exact J.superset_covering (Presheaf.equalizerSieve_self_eq_top _).ge (J.top_mem _)
492	    | tmul a kk =>
493	        rw [LinearMap.lTensor_tmul]
494	        have hk : ((toPresheaf _).map g).app X kk.1
495	            = ((toPresheaf _).map g).app X (0 : ((toPresheaf _).obj M).obj X) := by
496	          rw [map_zero]
497	          erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
498	          exact kk.2
499	        refine J.superset_covering ?_ (hg.equalizerSieve_mem kk.1 0 hk)

500	        intro V f hf
501	        rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf ⊢
502	        rw [map_zero] at hf ⊢
503	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
504	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe] at hf
505	        rw [Submodule.subtype_apply, hf]
506	        erw [TensorProduct.tmul_zero]; rfl
507	    | add ζ₁ ζ₂ h₁ h₂ =>
508	        rw [map_add]
509	        refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.intersection_covering h₁ h₂)
510	        intro V f hf
511	        obtain ⟨hf1, hf2⟩ := hf
512	        rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf1 hf2 ⊢
513	        rw [map_zero] at hf1 hf2 ⊢
514	        exact (map_add _ _ _).trans (by rw [hf1, hf2, add_zero])
515	  -- Transport `equalizerSieve (ξ - η) 0 ∈ J` to `equalizerSieve ξ η ∈ J`.
516	  have hmain : Presheaf.equalizerSieve (F := (toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)) (X := X)
517	      (ξ - η) 0 ∈ J X.unop := hζ ▸ key ζ
518	  refine J.superset_covering ?_ hmain
519	  intro V f hf
520	  rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf ⊢
521	  rw [map_zero, map_sub, sub_eq_zero] at hf
522	  exact hf

525	(Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`.) For a sectionwise-flat presheaf of
526	modules `F` and a morphism `g` lying in the sheafification localizer `J.W` (the
527	class of morphisms inverted by sheafification, equivalently the locally bijective
528	ones via `WEqualsLocallyBijective`), the left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` again
529	lies in `J.W`. The two halves are `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat` (where
530	flatness enters) and `isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft` (pure right-exactness).
531	This is the single non-formal ingredient of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
532	`tensorObj_assoc_iso`; the route uses no `MonoidalClosed` structure. -/
533	lemma W_whiskerLeft_of_flat [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
534	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
535	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
536	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
537	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
538	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) := by
539	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hg ⊢
540	  exact ⟨isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg.1,
541	    isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft F g hg.2⟩
542	
543	/-- **Flat right-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
544	braiding-conjugate of `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`: for a sectionwise-flat presheaf of
545	modules `F` and a morphism `g` in the sheafification localizer `J.W`, the
546	right-whiskered morphism `g ▷ F` again lies in `J.W`. Obtained from the
547	left-whiskered statement by conjugating with the (iso) braiding of the symmetric
548	presheaf-of-modules monoidal structure, using that `J.W` respects isomorphisms. -/
549	lemma W_whiskerRight_of_flat [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
550	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
551	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
552	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
553	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
554	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (g ▷ F)) := by
555	  have hwl := W_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg
556	  -- `g ▷ F = (β_ M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (β_ N F).inv` by braiding naturality.
557	  have hconj : g ▷ F
558	      = (BraidedCategory.braiding M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (BraidedCategory.braiding N F).inv := by
559	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_left g F, Category.assoc,
560	      Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
561	  rw [hconj, Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp]
562	  -- `J.W` respects isos on both sides (it is the sheafification localizer).
563	  rw [(J.W).cancel_left_of_respectsIso, (J.W).cancel_right_of_respectsIso]
564	  exact hwl

567	bijective morphism (ROUTE (d), the live associator realization)
568

570	flatness instance `∀ U, Module.Flat (R(U)) (F(U))`, which is FALSE for invertible

574	injective. Whiskering a *locally bijective* `g` by an *arbitrary* `F` preserves

577	the topological site, `Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW` / `TopCat.hasEnoughPoints`), the
578	tensor `id ⊗ g_x` is again an isomorphism — no flatness, because *isomorphisms*
579	tensor to isomorphisms whereas mere *injections* need flatness. This is exactly the
580	flatness-free technique Mathlib blesses for `J.W.IsMonoidal` via enough points
581	(analogist `ts-monoidal213.md`, route (d)). -/

703	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map
704	(ROUTE (e), ingredient d.1)
705	
706	The route-(e) residual `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` is a stalkwise
707	argument: a `J.W`-morphism `g` is a *stalkwise isomorphism*, so `(F ◁ g)_x =
708	id_{F_x} ⊗_{R_x} g_x` is again an isomorphism for arbitrary `F`. The stalkwise
709	characterisation it ultimately rests on (ingredient d.1) requires the induced
710	Ab-stalk map of a morphism `g : M ⟶ N` of presheaves of `R`-modules to be packaged
711	as an **`R.stalk x`-linear map** between the stalk modules.
712	
713	Mathlib (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`) already supplies, for `X : TopCat`
714	and `R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat`, the stalk module structure
715	`Module (R.stalk x) ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x)` together with the germ /
716	scalar compatibility `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`; what it does **not** supply is the
717	linearity of the induced stalk map `(stalkFunctor Ab x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)`.
718	This section provides that packaging (the first concrete ingredient of d.1 toward
719	`isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`). The base ring presheaf is necessarily
720	`CommRingCat`-valued, matching the project's `X.presheaf` carrier. -/
721	
722	section StalkLinearMap
723	
724	open TopologicalSpace TopCat.Presheaf Opposite
725	
726	variable {X : TopCat.{u}} {R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat.{u}}
727	
728	/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map of a morphism of presheaves of modules.**
729	For `g : M ⟶ N` in `PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)` over a topological space
730	`X` and a point `x`, the induced Ab-stalk map `(stalkFunctor Ab x).map
731	((toPresheaf _).map g) : M.presheaf.stalk x ⟶ N.presheaf.stalk x` is `R.stalk x`-linear
732	for the stalk module structures of `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`.
733	Project-local: Mathlib packages the stalk module structure (`germ_smul`) but not the
734	linearity of the induced stalk map. This is ingredient (d.1) of the route-(e)
735	stalkwise argument for `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`. -/
736	noncomputable def stalkLinearMap
737	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X) :
738	    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x) : Type u) →ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)]
739	      (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk N.presheaf x) : Type u) where
740	  toFun := (ConcreteCategory.hom
741	    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
742	  map_add' a b := map_add _ a b
743	  map_smul' r s := by
744	    dsimp only [RingHom.id_apply]
745	    obtain ⟨U, hxU, r₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist R x r
746	    obtain ⟨V, hxV, s₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist M.presheaf x s
747	    set W : Opens X := U ⊓ V with hW
748	    have hxW : x ∈ W := ⟨hxU, hxV⟩
749	    set iWU : W ⟶ U := homOfLE inf_le_left
750	    set iWV : W ⟶ V := homOfLE inf_le_right
751	    have hr : (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ U x hxU)) r₀
752	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ W x hxW)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map iWU.op)) r₀) :=
753	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply R iWU x hxW r₀).symm
754	    have hs : (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf V x hxV)) s₀
755	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W x hxW))
756	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (M.presheaf.map iWV.op)) s₀) :=
757	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply M.presheaf iWV x hxW s₀).symm
758	    have key : ∀ (z : (↑(M.obj (op W)) : Type u)),
759	        (ConcreteCategory.hom ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map
760	            ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
761	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W x hxW)) z)
762	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf W x hxW))
763	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) z) := by
764	      intro z
765	      rw [show (ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) z
766	            = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((toPresheaf _).map g).app (op W))) z from
767	            (toPresheaf_map_app_apply g (op W) z).symm]
768	      exact TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply (F := M.presheaf) (G := N.presheaf)
769	        W x hxW ((toPresheaf _).map g) z
770	    rw [hr, hs, ← PresheafOfModules.germ_smul M x W hxW, key, map_smul,
771	        PresheafOfModules.germ_smul N x W hxW, key]
772	
773	/-- **Germ characterisation of `stalkLinearMap`.** On the germ of a section `s` over
774	an open `U ∋ x`, `stalkLinearMap g x` is the germ of `g.app (op U) s`. This is the
775	defining naturality of the stalk map, exposed for the downstream d.2 assembly
776	(identifying the stalk map of `F ◁ g` with `id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x`). -/
777	lemma stalkLinearMap_germ
778	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
779	    (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U) (s : (↑(M.obj (op U)) : Type u)) :
780	    stalkLinearMap g x ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf U x hx)) s)
781	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf U x hx))
782	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op U))) s) := by
783	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map
784	        ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
785	      ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf U x hx)) s) = _
786	  rw [show (ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op U))) s
787	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((toPresheaf _).map g).app (op U))) s from
788	        (toPresheaf_map_app_apply g (op U) s).symm]
789	  exact TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply (F := M.presheaf) (G := N.presheaf)
790	    U x hx ((toPresheaf _).map g) s
791	
792	/-- **A stalkwise-iso morphism induces a bijective `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map.**
793	If the underlying Ab-stalk map of `g` at `x` is an isomorphism, then `stalkLinearMap
794	g x` is bijective — hence (being `R.stalk x`-linear) an `R.stalk x`-linear
795	equivalence `M_x ≃ₗ N_x`. This is the form ingredient (d.1) feeds into the
796	`id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` step (tensoring an `R.stalk x`-linear equivalence by `id` stays an
797	equivalence, flatness-free). -/
798	lemma stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso
799	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
800	    (h : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g))) :
801	    Function.Bijective (stalkLinearMap g x) := by
802	  change Function.Bijective ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom
803	    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
804	  exact ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso _
805	
806	/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear stalk equivalence of a stalkwise-iso morphism.** When the
807	underlying Ab-stalk map of `g` at `x` is an isomorphism, `stalkLinearMap g x` upgrades
808	to an `R.stalk x`-linear equivalence `M_x ≃ₗ N_x`. This is the exact object the route-(e)
809	`id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` step consumes: tensoring it by `id_{F_x}` (`LinearEquiv.lTensor`) yields
810	an equivalence with no flatness hypothesis. -/
811	noncomputable def stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso
812	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
813	    (h : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g))) :
814	    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x) : Type u) ≃ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)]
815	      (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk N.presheaf x) : Type u) :=
816	  LinearEquiv.ofBijective (stalkLinearMap g x) (stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso g x h)
817	
818	end StalkLinearMap
819	
820	end PresheafOfModules

983	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the internal hom of presheaves of modules
984	(slice formula): the `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)`
985	
986	This section builds the FIRST primitive of the sheaf internal-hom / dual block
987	(blueprint `sec:tensorobj_dual_infra`, the `⊗`-inverse's missing ingredient): the
988	`R(T)`-module structure on the morphism abelian group `M ⟶ N` of presheaves of
989	modules over a base category `C` with a **terminal object** `T`, where the scalar
990	ring is the global ring `R(T)`.
991	
992	This is exactly the module attached to each value of the slice internal hom
993	`ℋom(M, N)(U) := ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)` of
994	blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`: over the restricted site (terminal `U`),
995	the section module over `U` is `Hom(M|_U, N|_U)` with its `R(U)`-action. The slice
996	formula is forced by contravariance of the naive pointwise rule
997	`U ↦ Hom_{R(U)}(M(U), N(U))`; the module of morphisms of *restricted* objects is
998	the covariant remedy, and its `R(U)`-module structure is the content here.
999	
1000	The action is `f • φ := φ ≫ globalSMul f`, where `globalSMul f : N ⟶ N` is the
1001	"multiply by the global scalar `f ∈ R(T)`" endomorphism: at an object `Y`, with
1002	`R(T) → R(Y)` the canonical map `termRingMap` induced by the unique `Y → T`, it is
1003	scalar multiplication by the image of `f`. Mathlib has the fixed-ring internal hom
1004	`ihom M N = (M ⟶ N)` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Monoidal/Closed.lean`) but
1005	nothing for the varying structure sheaf at the `PresheafOfModules` level; this is the
1006	project-local supplement. -/
1007	
1008	namespace InternalHom
1009	
1010	open CategoryTheory Limits
1011	
1012	universe vC uC
1013	
1014	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{vC} C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1015	  {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T)
1016	
1017	/-- **The canonical ring map `R(T) → R(Y)` from a terminal object `T`.** For each
1018	object `Y`, the unique morphism `Y.unop → T` (terminality) induces, after `op` and
1019	applying `R`, the ring map `R(T) → R(Y)` along which a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` acts
1020	on `R(Y)`-modules. Project-local: the `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)` (the slice
1021	internal-hom value) is defined through this map. -/
1022	noncomputable def termRingMap (Y : Cᵒᵖ) : R.obj (Opposite.op T) ⟶ R.obj Y :=
1023	  R.map (hT.from Y.unop).op
1024	
1025	/-- **Naturality of `termRingMap`.** The restriction map `R(g) : R(X) → R(Y)` of `R`
1026	along `g : X ⟶ Y` carries `termRingMap X f` to `termRingMap Y f`; equivalently the
1027	images of a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` are compatible with restriction. This is the
1028	sole ingredient making `globalSMul f` a genuine morphism of presheaves of modules. -/
1029	lemma termRingMap_naturality {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (g : X ⟶ Y) (f) :
1030	    (ConcreteCategory.hom ((R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map g))
1031	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT X)) f)
1032	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f := by
1033	  have h : (hT.from X.unop).op ≫ g = (hT.from Y.unop).op := by
1034	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj; apply hT.hom_ext
1035	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map g)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map (hT.from X.unop).op)) f) = _
1036	  rw [show termRingMap hT Y = R.map (hT.from Y.unop).op from rfl, ← h, Functor.map_comp]; rfl
1037	
1038	/-- **Multiplication by a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` as an endomorphism of `N`.** At an
1039	object `Y`, it is scalar multiplication by `termRingMap Y f ∈ R(Y)` on `N(Y)`; the
1040	naturality square commutes by `termRingMap_naturality` and the semilinearity of the
1041	restriction maps of `N` (`PresheafOfModules.map_smul`). This is the central object: it
1042	gives the `R(T)`-action on `Hom(M, N)` by post-composition (`homModule`). -/
1043	noncomputable def globalSMul (N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1044	    (f : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) : N ⟶ N :=
1045	  PresheafOfModules.Hom.mk
1046	    (fun Y => ModuleCat.ofHom
1047	      (LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj Y : Type u) (N.obj Y) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f)))
1048	    (by
1049	      intro X Y g; ext m
1050	      change (LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj Y : Type u) (N.obj Y)
1051	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (N.map g)) m)
1052	          = (ConcreteCategory.hom (N.map g)) ((LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj X : Type u) (N.obj X)
1053	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT X)) f)) m)
1054	      rw [LinearMap.lsmul_apply, LinearMap.lsmul_apply]; erw [PresheafOfModules.map_smul]
1055	      rw [termRingMap_naturality]; rfl)
1056	
1057	variable {N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1058	
1059	/-- The section-wise action of `globalSMul f`: at `Y`, it is scalar multiplication by
1060	`termRingMap Y f`. -/
1061	lemma globalSMul_hom_apply (f : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) (Y : Cᵒᵖ) (m : N.obj Y) :
1062	    ((globalSMul hT N f).app Y).hom m = ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f) • m := rfl
1063	
1064	/-- `globalSMul 1 = 𝟙`. -/
1065	lemma globalSMul_one : globalSMul hT N 1 = 𝟙 N := by
1066	  ext Y m; rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, map_one, one_smul]; rfl
1067	
1068	/-- `globalSMul 0 = 0`. -/
1069	lemma globalSMul_zero : globalSMul hT N 0 = 0 := by
1070	  ext Y m; rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, map_zero, zero_smul]; rfl
1071	
1072	/-- `globalSMul` is additive in the scalar. -/
1073	lemma globalSMul_add (f g : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) :
1074	    globalSMul hT N (f + g) = globalSMul hT N f + globalSMul hT N g := by
1075	  ext Y m; rw [add_app, globalSMul_hom_apply, _root_.map_add, add_smul]; rfl
1076	
1077	/-- `globalSMul` turns ring multiplication into composition (the scalar endomorphisms
1078	commute, so the order is immaterial). -/
1079	lemma globalSMul_mul (f g : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) :
1080	    globalSMul hT N (f * g) = globalSMul hT N g ≫ globalSMul hT N f := by
1081	  ext Y m; rw [comp_app, globalSMul_hom_apply, map_mul, mul_smul, ModuleCat.hom_comp,
1082	    LinearMap.comp_apply, globalSMul_hom_apply, globalSMul_hom_apply]
1083	
1084	/-- **The `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)` of presheaves of modules over a base
1085	category `C` with a terminal object `T`.** The action `f • φ := φ ≫ globalSMul f` scales
1086	a morphism by post-composing with multiplication by the global scalar `f ∈ R(T)`; the
1087	module axioms reduce to the `globalSMul_{one,zero,add,mul}` ring-homomorphism facts and
1088	the bilinearity of composition (`Preadditive`). This is the module carried by each value
1089	of the slice internal hom `ℋom(M, N)(U)` of blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`
1090	(take `C =` the restricted site over `U`, with terminal `U`, so `R(T) = R(U)`).
1091	Project-local: Mathlib has the fixed-ring internal hom but no `PresheafOfModules`-level
1092	internal hom for the varying structure sheaf. -/
1093	@[implicit_reducible]
1094	noncomputable def homModule (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1095	    Module (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u) (M ⟶ N) where
1096	  smul f φ := φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f
1097	  one_smul φ := by change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N 1 = φ; rw [globalSMul_one, Category.comp_id]
1098	  mul_smul f g φ := by
1099	    change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N (f * g) = (φ ≫ globalSMul hT N g) ≫ globalSMul hT N f

1100	    rw [globalSMul_mul, Category.assoc]
1101	  smul_zero f := by change (0 : M ⟶ N) ≫ globalSMul hT N f = 0; rw [Limits.zero_comp]
1102	  zero_smul φ := by change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N 0 = 0; rw [globalSMul_zero, Limits.comp_zero]
1103	  smul_add f φ ψ := by
1104	    change (φ + ψ) ≫ globalSMul hT N f = φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f + ψ ≫ globalSMul hT N f
1105	    rw [Preadditive.add_comp]
1106	  add_smul f g φ := by
1107	    change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N (f + g) = φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f + φ ≫ globalSMul hT N g
1108	    rw [globalSMul_add, Preadditive.comp_add]
1109	
1110	/-! ### The slice value at an object `U` via the over-category `Over U`
1111	
1112	The slice internal hom `ℋom(M, N)(U) := ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)` of
1113	blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom` is realized object-by-object here. The
1114	restriction `M|_U` is `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀` along the over-category
1115	projection `Over.forget U : Over U ⥤ C`; the over-category has terminal object
1116	`Over.mk (𝟙 U)` (`Over.mkIdTerminal`), at which the restricted ring is `R(U)`
1117	(by `rfl`), so the `R(U)`-module structure on `M|_U ⟶ N|_U` is exactly `homModule`
1118	applied to that terminal. -/
1119	
1120	/-- **Restriction of a presheaf of modules to the over-category `Over U`.** This is
1121	the `M|_U` of the slice internal-hom formula: `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀` along
1122	the over-category projection `Over.forget U`. The new base presheaf of rings is
1123	`(Over.forget U).op ⋙ R`, whose value at the terminal `Over.mk (𝟙 U)` is `R(U)`. -/
1124	noncomputable def restr (U : C)
1125	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1126	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (((Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1127	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U) (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).obj M
1128	
1129	/-- **The slice internal-hom value at `U`: `Hom(M|_U, N|_U)` as an `R(U)`-module.**
1130	This is the `R(U)`-module underlying `ℋom(M, N)(U)` of blueprint
1131	`def:presheaf_internal_hom`: the morphism group of the over-category restrictions
1132	`M|_U ⟶ N|_U`, equipped with the `homModule` action for the terminal object
1133	`Over.mk (𝟙 U)` of `Over U`. The full presheaf `internalHom` (the assembly of these
1134	values over the restriction maps `V ⟶ U`) was assembled iter-220 (`internalHom`). -/
1135	@[implicit_reducible]
1136	noncomputable def internalHomObjModule (U : C)
1137	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1138	    Module (R.obj (Opposite.op U) : Type u) (restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :=
1139	  homModule (R := (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 U)) Over.mkIdTerminal
1140	    (restr U M) (restr U N)
1141	
1142	/-- **The restriction map of the presheaf internal hom.** For a morphism `g : V ⟶ U`
1143	of `C`, restricting a morphism `φ : M|_U ⟶ N|_U` of restricted presheaves of modules
1144	along `Over.map g : Over V ⥤ Over U` yields a morphism `M|_V ⟶ N|_V`. Realised as
1145	`(pushforward₀ (Over.map g) …).map φ`; the target base ring presheaf
1146	`(Over.map g).op ⋙ (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R` is definitionally `(Over.forget V).op ⋙ R`,
1147	so the result has the expected type `restr V M ⟶ restr V N`. This is the
1148	further-restriction map `φ ↦ φ|_V` of blueprint `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`. -/
1149	noncomputable def restrictionMap {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1150	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1151	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) : restr V M ⟶ restr V N :=
1152	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.map g)
1153	    ((Over.forget U).op ⋙ (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map φ
1154	
1155	/-- **`restrictionMap` is additive.** Part of the additivity assertion of blueprint
1156	`lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`. -/
1157	lemma restrictionMap_add {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1158	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1159	    (φ ψ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
1160	    restrictionMap g (φ + ψ) = restrictionMap g φ + restrictionMap g ψ := by
1161	  ext1 X; rfl
1162	
1163	/-- **`restrictionMap` preserves zero.** -/
1164	lemma restrictionMap_zero {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1165	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)} :
1166	    restrictionMap g (0 : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) = 0 := by
1167	  ext1 X; rfl
1168	
1169	/-- Helper: the component of a morphism of presheaves of modules at two *equal*
1170	objects agrees up to `HEq`. Proven by `subst`. Used to discharge the
1171	pseudofunctoriality coherence of `restrictionMap` (`Over.map` is only functorial in
1172	its argument up to `Over.mapId_eq` / `Over.mapComp_eq`). -/
1173	private lemma hom_app_heq {B : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} B}
1174	    (φ : M ⟶ N) {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (h : X = Y) : HEq (φ.app X) (φ.app Y) := by
1175	  subst h; rfl
1176	
1177	/-- **Functoriality of `restrictionMap`: identity.** -/
1178	lemma restrictionMap_id {U : C}
1179	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1180	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
1181	    restrictionMap (𝟙 U) φ = φ := by
1182	  ext1 X
1183	  exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (by rw [Over.mapId_eq]; rfl))
1184	
1185	/-- **Functoriality of `restrictionMap`: composition.** For `g : V ⟶ U`, `h : W ⟶ V`,
1186	restricting along `h ≫ g` is restricting along `g` then along `h`. -/
1187	lemma restrictionMap_comp {U V W : C} (g : V ⟶ U) (h : W ⟶ V)
1188	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1189	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
1190	    restrictionMap (h ≫ g) φ = restrictionMap h (restrictionMap g φ) := by
1191	  ext1 X
1192	  exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (by rw [Over.mapComp_eq]; rfl))
1193	
1194	/-- **`restrictionMap` respects composition of morphisms** (functoriality of
1195	`pushforward₀.map`). -/
1196	lemma restrictionMap_comp_hom {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1197	    {M N P : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1198	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) (ψ : restr U N ⟶ restr U P) :
1199	    restrictionMap g (φ ≫ ψ) = restrictionMap g φ ≫ restrictionMap g ψ :=
1200	  Functor.map_comp _ _ _
1201	
1202	/-- **Restriction commutes with the global-scalar endomorphism.** For `g : V ⟶ U`
1203	and a global scalar `r ∈ R(U)`, restricting the multiplication-by-`r` endomorphism of
1204	`N|_U` yields the multiplication-by-`R(g)(r)` endomorphism of `N|_V`. This is the heart
1205	of the semilinearity of `restrictionMap` (blueprint `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`):
1206	both sides act on each section by scalar multiplication, and the scalars agree by
1207	functoriality of `R` (`termRingMap_naturality`). -/
1208	lemma restrictionMap_globalSMul {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1209	    {N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1210	    (r : (R.obj (Opposite.op U) : Type u)) :
1211	    restrictionMap g (globalSMul (R := (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 U))
1212	        Over.mkIdTerminal (restr U N) r)
1213	      = globalSMul (R := (Over.forget V).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 V))
1214	        Over.mkIdTerminal (restr V N) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map g.op)) r) := by
1215	  ext1 Y
1216	  ext m
1217	  rw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
1218	  erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
1219	  congr 1
1220	  simp only [termRingMap, Functor.comp_map]
1221	  have hmor : (Over.forget U).op.map
1222	        (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).op.obj Y).unop).op
1223	      = g.op ≫ (Over.forget V).op.map (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op := by
1224	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj
1225	    change Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop))
1226	      = Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop) ≫ g
1227	    have e1 : Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop))
1228	        = ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop).hom := by simp
1229	    have e2 : Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop) = Y.unop.hom := by simp
1230	    rw [e1, e2]
1231	    simp [Over.map_obj_hom]
1232	  rw [hmor]
1233	  erw [← CommRingCat.comp_apply, ← R.map_comp]
1234	  rfl
1235	
1236	/-- **`restrictionMap` packaged as an additive group homomorphism** on the morphism
1237	groups, using `restrictionMap_zero` and `restrictionMap_add`. This is the value of the
1238	underlying `Ab`-presheaf morphism of `internalHom`. -/
1239	noncomputable def restrictionMapAddHom {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1240	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1241	    (restr U M ⟶ restr U N) →+ (restr V M ⟶ restr V N) where
1242	  toFun := restrictionMap g
1243	  map_zero' := restrictionMap_zero g
1244	  map_add' := restrictionMap_add g
1245	
1246	/-- **The underlying `Ab`-valued presheaf of `internalHom M N`**: `U ↦ (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)`
1247	with the further-restriction maps. Functoriality is `restrictionMap_id` /
1248	`restrictionMap_comp`. This is piece (a)+(b)+(c) of blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`
1249	at the abelian-group level; the `R(U)`-module refinement is added by `internalHom`. -/
1250	noncomputable def internalHomPresheaf
1251	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1252	    Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{max u uC vC} where
1253	  obj X := AddCommGrpCat.of (restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop N)
1254	  map {X Y} f := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (restrictionMapAddHom f.unop M N)
1255	  map_id X := by
1256	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
1257	    refine AddMonoidHom.ext fun φ => ?_
1258	    exact restrictionMap_id φ
1259	  map_comp {X Y Z} f f' := by
1260	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
1261	    refine AddMonoidHom.ext fun φ => ?_
1262	    exact restrictionMap_comp f.unop f'.unop φ
1263	
1264	/-- **Semilinearity of `restrictionMap`** in the form consumed by
1265	`PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf`: for `f : X ⟶ Y` in `Cᵒᵖ`, a global scalar
1266	`r ∈ R(X)` and `m : M|_{X} ⟶ N|_{X}`, `restrictionMap f.unop (r • m) =
1267	R(f)(r) • restrictionMap f.unop m`, where the `•`'s are the slice-value module
1268	actions (`internalHomObjModule`). This is `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`'s
1269	compatibility datum; it follows from `restrictionMap_comp_hom` and
1270	`restrictionMap_globalSMul`. -/
1271	lemma restrictionMap_smul {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y)
1272	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1273	    (r : (R.obj X : Type u)) (m : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop N) :
1274	    restrictionMap f.unop
1275	        (letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop M N; r • m)
1276	      = (letI := internalHomObjModule Y.unop M N;
1277	          (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map f)) r • restrictionMap f.unop m) := by
1278	  change restrictionMap f.unop (m ≫ globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal (restr X.unop N) r)
1279	    = restrictionMap f.unop m
1280	        ≫ globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal (restr Y.unop N) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map f)) r)
1281	  rw [restrictionMap_comp_hom, restrictionMap_globalSMul, Quiver.Hom.op_unop]
1282	
1283	end InternalHom

1285	/-! ### Assembly of the presheaf internal hom over a single-universe base
1286	
1287	`PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf` ties the underlying `Ab`-presheaf to the ground ring
1288	presheaf `R`'s universe (`Type u`), but for a general base category the morphism groups
1289	`M|_U ⟶ N|_U` live in `Type (max u uC vC)`. The two coincide exactly when the base
1290	category is single-universe (`Category.{u, u}`), which is the case for the topological
1291	site `Opens X` underlying the structure sheaf of a scheme. The full presheaf internal
1292	hom is therefore assembled in this specialised universe context. -/
1293	
1294	namespace InternalHom
1295	
1296	section Assembly
1297	
1298	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1299

1300	/-- **The presheaf internal hom `ℋom(M, N)`** (blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`):
1301	the presheaf of `R`-modules assembled from the slice value modules
1302	`internalHomObjModule` (a), the further-restriction maps `internalHomPresheaf` (b)(c),
1303	and the semilinearity datum `restrictionMap_smul`, via `PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf`.
1304	Stated over a single-universe base (e.g. the topological site `Opens X`); see the
1305	section note for the universe constraint. -/
1306	noncomputable def internalHom
1307	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1308	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1309	  @PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf D _ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
1310	    (internalHomPresheaf M N)
1311	    (fun X => internalHomObjModule X.unop M N)
1312	    (fun {_ _} f r m => restrictionMap_smul f M N r m)
1313	
1314	end Assembly
1315	
1316	end InternalHom

1320	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite
1321	
1322	universe vC uC
1323	
1324	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{vC} C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1325	  {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T)
1326	
1327	/-- **`termRingMap` at the terminal object itself is the identity.** The unique
1328	`T → T` is the identity, so the induced ring map `R(T) → R(T)` is `id`. This is the
